# ppmlx

> MLX-native LLM inference server for Apple Silicon. Open source. MIT license.

ppmlx runs large language models natively on Apple Silicon using Apple's MLX framework. It provides an OpenAI-compatible API on localhost, making it a drop-in replacement for any tool that speaks the OpenAI protocol.

## Key facts

- 47-125% faster than Ollama (GGUF translation layer) on Apple Silicon benchmarks
- 168+ curated models with human aliases (Llama, Qwen, Mistral, Phi, Gemma, DeepSeek, etc.)
- OpenAI Chat Completions API, Responses API, Completions API, Embeddings API
- Anthropic Messages API (for Claude Code compatibility)
- Tool calling / function calling support (XML and JSON formats)
- Vision model support via mlx-vlm
- Interactive TUI model picker and chat REPL
- Modelfile support (Ollama-compatible syntax)
- SQLite request logging
- Zero config required. Runs on localhost:6767.

## Install

```
uv tool install ppmlx
```

Requires macOS on Apple Silicon (M1/M2/M3/M4), Python 3.11+.

## Quick start

```
ppmlx pull qwen3.5
ppmlx serve --model qwen3.5
```

Then connect any OpenAI-compatible client to http://localhost:6767/v1 with any API key.

## Links

- Website: https://ppmlx.dev
- GitHub: https://github.com/the-focus-company/ppmlx
- PyPI: https://pypi.org/project/ppmlx/
- Issues: https://github.com/the-focus-company/ppmlx/issues

## Benchmarks (GLM-4.7-Flash, MacBook Pro M4 Pro 48GB, 3 runs averaged)

| Scenario | ppmlx tok/s | Ollama tok/s | Delta |
|----------|-------------|--------------|-------|
| Simple   | 61.6        | 41.8         | +47%  |
| Complex  | 56.1        | 38.7         | +45%  |
| Agentic  | 46.7        | 20.8         | +125% |

## API Endpoints

- POST /v1/chat/completions — Chat with streaming, tools, vision
- POST /v1/responses — Responses API (Codex, newer OpenAI tools)
- POST /v1/messages — Anthropic Messages API (Claude Code)
- POST /v1/embeddings — Vector embeddings
- POST /v1/completions — Text completion
- GET /v1/models — List available models
- GET /health — Server health and memory usage

## Built by

The Focus Company (https://thefocus.company)
